What about Ma and Pa America’s optics.  What about the silent majority of you at home who just want safe neighborhoods?

Secure the country for the people who actually built it.

author.description: Retired Border Patrol Commander-at-Large. Spent 30 years doing the job politicians only campaign on. Now calling out their failures and offering real solutions.

tweets.text:
History lesson: record deportations from the Obama years that were straight-up cooked books. 

Barack merely relabeled apprehensions at the border as formal removals to juice the numbers. See for yourself:

•House Judiciary on the bookkeeping tricks: https://t.co/ZJRNCl3aBd (2012 press release detailing internal ICE docs and the ATEP program shift)
•Jeh Johnson himself admitting it in Congress: https://t.co/VBnWw7gibY (covering the 2014 House hearing where Johnson confirmed to Rep. Culberson that most ICE removals were recent Border Patrol apprehensions not counted the same way in prior administrations). 

Meanwhile, the current approach has resulted in an end to self-deportations because when enforcement is serious, people actually leave on their own.
tweets.text_resolved:
History lesson: record deportations from the Obama years that were straight-up cooked books. 

Barack merely relabeled apprehensions at the border as formal removals to juice the numbers. See for yourself:

•House Judiciary on the bookkeeping tricks: http://judiciary.house.gov/media/press-releases/smith-administration-cooks-the-books-to-achieve-deportation-numbers (2012 press release detailing internal ICE docs and the ATEP program shift)
•Jeh Johnson himself admitting it in Congress: https://www.politifact.com/factchecks/2014/jul/15/lou-dobbs/lou-dobbs-obama-administration-manipulated-deporta/ (covering the 2014 House hearing where Johnson confirmed to Rep. Culberson that most ICE removals were recent Border Patrol apprehensions not counted the same way in prior administrations). 

Meanwhile, the current approach has resulted in an end to self-deportations because when enforcement is serious, people actually leave on their own.

author.description: Retired Border Patrol Commander-at-Large. Spent 30 years doing the job politicians only campaign on. Now calling out their failures and offering real solutions.

tweets.text:
The quest for cheap labor isn’t an “economic policy.”
It’s a system designed to import the cheapest possible bodies while ignoring the predictable body count on the American side. 

Exhibit A:

Colombian national Deiby Jhonatan Janamejoy Jansasoy sneaks across the border, gets released with no vetting, blows off his court date, gets a final deportation order… and three weeks later crosses the centerline drunk and murders 47-year-old American dad Michael Sharpe in front of his family.  As a border patrol executive, I always thought in terms of risk management. Our political leaders have supported a system that enables this, and it’s in direct contradiction to basic risk management. Michael wasn’t “replaced” by cheaper labor. He was erased by it. 

Every time a politician or CEO smiles and says “we need the labor,” remember the Sharpe kids growing up without their father.

That’s not a side effect.

That’s the system working exactly as designed.  Cheap labor. Expensive American lives!

author.description: Retired Border Patrol Commander-at-Large. Spent 30 years doing the job politicians only campaign on. Now calling out their failures and offering real solutions.

tweets.text:
History lesson for every American patriot:

Border Patrol used to have interior offices all over the country — Las Vegas, Little Rock, Sacramento, Stockton, Livermore, Amarillo, and more.  Every president slowly shut them down and moved the agents to the border.

The message became clear: get 100 miles north and you’re basically home free.

Drive through any community along our southern border today , you won’t see illegal aliens there because they fear the Mean Green Team will round them up.  That’s exactly how enforcement should work everywhere.

Imagine if interior enforcement had never stopped. Would we have the hundred million here today?

This wasn’t an accident, it was policy. Time to fix it.

And how are those self deportation numbers looking??? https://t.co/dwOvuDzNkM
tweets.text_resolved:

author.description: Retired Border Patrol Commander-at-Large. Spent 30 years doing the job politicians only campaign on. Now calling out their failures and offering real solutions.

tweets.text:
Look, I get it. A lot of us are demoralized watching RINOs and Chamber of Commerce types run for office. They talk tough but sell us out for cheap labor and open borders every single time.

But here’s the brutal truth: sitting home or splitting votes isn’t an option.

This midterm, we vote the RINOs out and replace them with people of real substance — fighters who actually stop immigration .

If we don’t act and Democrats regain power, they will flood us with forty million more illegal aliens, which they will then legalize. Then the country is lost forever.

This is the hill. 

Stand. 

Vote like your future depends on it. 

Because it does.
tweets.text_resolved:
Look, I get it. A lot of us are demoralized watching RINOs and Chamber of Commerce types run for office. They talk tough but sell us out for cheap labor and open borders every single time.

If we don’t act and Democrats regain power, they will flood us with forty million more illegal aliens, which they will then legalize. Then the country is lost forever.

This is the hill. 

Stand. 

Vote like your future depends on it. 

Because it does.

author.description: Retired Border Patrol Commander-at-Large. Spent 30 years doing the job politicians only campaign on. Now calling out their failures and offering real solutions.

tweets.text:
I’m proud to endorse @AnthonySabatini Sabatini for Congress in Florida’s 11th District.
Imagine America five years from now if we keep treating our country like nothing more than an economic zone — a giant open-air Walmart with no borders, no identity, and no loyalty to the people who built it. Picture overwhelmed schools where your kids get less attention, hospitals turning away citizens because beds are filled with emergency cases that never end, wages stuck or falling while housing prices explode, and neighborhoods that feel less safe every single year. That’s not a country anymore. That’s a business that’s liquidating its own citizens.

Sabatini gets it: the fight against illegal immigration isn’t about politics — it’s about whether America remains a real country with sovereignty, culture, and a future for our kids, or just a giant hotel with no checkout time. He’s willing to say what most won’t: secure borders are the foundation of everything else that matters.

That’s why he has my full support. The choice is clear — a sovereign America, or something much worse. Sabatini is fighting for the first one."
— Greg Bovino
